title: K-ε-GG Peptide Enrichment and Analysis by Tandem Mass Tagging-based Proteomics
author: Harper JW
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.buyunxww
materials: One IP per sample. For one IP: 32 µg of a-K-ε-GG Antibody per 40 µL slurry (Pierce™ Protein A Plus UltraLink™ Resin, Cat. No. 53142) - see below for coupling of antibody to resin. Add the resin to a 15 mL Eppendorf tube.
a. Wash C-18 with 1 mL 100% ACN.
e. Collect flow through and freeze.
d. Load sample (1 drop per second).
b. Equilibrate with 3 mL of 1% FA.
Follow manufacturer’s instructions (Thermo Cat# 84868). Elution used: 17.5% ACN, 20% ACN, 22.5% ACN, 25% ACN, 27.5% ACN and 70% ACN.
Speed vac individual samples to dryness.

The analysis of K-ε-GG peptides by mass spectrometry will depend on the type of instrument/platform used. Typical instrument settings for analysis on a Thermo Fusion Lumos instrument are provided in the following section. Inject 3 µL for each LC–MS/MS analysis using available mass spectrometer with a 120-minute online LC separation.

Seperate peptides on a 100 micromolar (µM) inner diameter microcapillary column packed in house with ~ 35 cm of Accucore150 resin ( 2.6 micromolar (µM) , 150 Å, ThermoFisher Scientific, San Jose, CA) with a gradient consisting of 3%–26% (0-100 min), 26-32% (100-110min) (ACN, 0.1% FA) over a total 02:00:00 run at ~ 400 nL/min .

steps.title: Lyse cells in 3 mL of...
steps.instruction: Lyse cells in 3 mL of lysis buffer and pass through a 21G needle 10 times.

steps.title: Centrifuge suspensions at 13000 rpm, °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge suspensions at 13000 rpm, °C, (high speed) for 00:10:00 at 4 °C and collect supernatant.

steps.title: Quantify protein lysate concentration and transfer...
steps.instruction: Quantify protein lysate concentration and transfer 1 mg of lysates to a clean tube.

steps.title: Reduce lysates for 00:20:00 at Room...
steps.instruction: Reduce lysates for 00:20:00 at Room temperature with 5 millimolar (mM) TCEP.

steps.title: Alkylate cysteine residues with 20 millimolar...
steps.instruction: Alkylate cysteine residues with 20 millimolar (mM) Chloroacetamide ( Room temperature , 00:30:00 ).

steps.title: Extract protein content by methanol-chloroform precipitation and subsequent MetOH washes
steps.instruction: Extract protein content by methanol-chloroform precipitation and subsequent MetOH washes.

steps.title: Add 4x volumes of MeOH and vortex
steps.instruction: Add 4x volumes of MeOH and vortex.

steps.title: Dry protein pellet down to get rid of MetOH traces
steps.instruction: Dry protein pellet down to get rid of MetOH traces.

steps.title: Spin down at Room temperature for 00:05:00 at high speed
steps.instruction: Spin down at Room temperature for 00:05:00 at high speed.

steps.title: Add 4x volumes of MeOH and vortex
steps.instruction: Add 4x volumes of MeOH and vortex.

steps.title: Remove both the aqueous and organic layers carefully, discard
steps.instruction: Remove both the aqueous and organic layers carefully, discard.

steps.title: Spin down at Room temperature for 00:05:00 at high speed
steps.instruction: Spin down at Room temperature for 00:05:00 at high speed.

steps.title: Add 3x volume of water and vortex
steps.instruction: Add 3x volume of water and vortex.

steps.title: Add 1x volume of chloroform and vortex
steps.instruction: Add 1x volume of chloroform and vortex.

steps.title: Resuspend protein pellets in 8 Molarity...
steps.instruction: Resuspend protein pellets in 8 Molarity (M) urea, 50 millimolar (mM) EPPS ( pH 8.5 ) buffer.

steps.title: Dilute samples to 4 Molarity (M)...
steps.instruction: Dilute samples to 4 Molarity (M) urea with 50 millimolar (mM) EPPS ( pH 8.5 ) and digest at 30 °C for 02:00:00 with endoproteinase Lys-C (Wako, Japan) at a 1/200 enzyme/protein ratio.

steps.title: Dilute samples to 1 Molarity (M)...
steps.instruction: Dilute samples to 1 Molarity (M) urea with 50 millimolar (mM) EPPS ( pH 8.5 ).

steps.title: Digest with Trypsin (1:100) o/n at 37 °C
steps.instruction: Digest with Trypsin (1:100) o/n at 37 °C .

steps.title: Stop digestion by acidification with formic...
steps.instruction: Stop digestion by acidification with formic acid (FA) 5% (v/v) (pH ~ 2).

steps.title: Subject peptides to C18 SepPak solid-phase...
steps.instruction: Subject peptides to C18 SepPak solid-phase extraction cartridges (SPE Waters) and dry down.

steps.title: Resuspend the desalted peptides in 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend the desalted peptides in 1.3 mL IAP buffer.

steps.title: Wash 3x with PBS and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Wash 3x with PBS and centrifuge 1’ at 1000 x g, °C, .

steps.title: Add the a-K-ε-GG Antibody and add...
steps.instruction: Add the a-K-ε-GG Antibody and add enough PBS to have a total volume of 10 mL in 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Incubate O/N at 4 °C with gentle rotation
steps.instruction: Incubate O/N at 4 °C with gentle rotation.

steps.title: Wash the anti-K-ε-GG Antibody coupled beads...
steps.instruction: Wash the anti-K-ε-GG Antibody coupled beads 3x with 3 mL 100 millimolar (mM) sodium borate, pH 9.0.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads in 3 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads in 3 mL of 20 millimolar (mM) DMP in 100 millimolar (mM) sodium borate (pH 9.0) and incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 with gentle end-over-end rotator.

steps.title: Stop the reaction by washing the...
steps.instruction: Stop the reaction by washing the beads 2x with 3 mL of antibody blocking buffer ( 200 millimolar (mM) ethanolamine pH 8.0).

steps.title: Resuspend in 3 mL of Antibody...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 3 mL of Antibody blocking buffer and incubate the Ab for 02:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle rotation.

steps.title: Wash the cross-linked antibody 3x with 3 mL IAP buffer
steps.instruction: Wash the cross-linked antibody 3x with 3 mL IAP buffer.

steps.title: Add each sample to a clean...
steps.instruction: Add each sample to a clean 2 mL Eppendorf tube containing the cross-linked anti-K-ε-GG Antibody ( 40 µL slurry of resin).

steps.title: Incubate the IPs for 02:00:00 at...
steps.instruction: Incubate the IPs for 02:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle end-over-end rotation.

steps.title: Centrifuge each IP at 2000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge each IP at 2000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 and remove the supernatant. Store supernatant at -80 °C .

steps.title: Wash the beads 3x with 2...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads 3x with 2 mL of IAP buffer followed with a wash with 2 mL PBS.

steps.title: To elute K-ε-GG peptides add 75...
steps.instruction: To elute K-ε-GG peptides add 75 µL of elution solution (0.15% TFA), gently tap the bottom of the tube several times and let the tube stand at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Repeat elution step and combine both eluates
steps.instruction: Repeat elution step and combine both eluates.

steps.title: Dry down in speedvac and proceed to stage-tip
steps.instruction: Dry down in speedvac and proceed to stage-tip.

steps.title: Resuspend samples in 5% FA, 5% ACN
steps.instruction: Resuspend samples in 5% FA, 5% ACN.

steps.title: Perform C-18 cleanup:
steps.instruction: Perform C-18 cleanup:

steps.title: h
steps.instruction: h. Elute with 2 x 500 µL 75% ACN/1% FA.

steps.title: f
steps.instruction: f. Wash with 3 mL of 1% FA/5% ACN.

steps.title: Dry down in speedvac
steps.instruction: Dry down in speedvac.

steps.title: Resuspend the peptide pellet in 50...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the peptide pellet in 50 µL of 200 millimolar (mM) EPPS (pH 8.2) containing 20% ACN.

steps.title: Add 3 µL - 4 µL...
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL - 4 µL of the TMT reagent to each sample.

steps.title: Incubate for 01:00:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Stop the reaction with 4 µL of hydroxylamine 5%
steps.instruction: Stop the reaction with 4 µL of hydroxylamine 5%.

steps.title: Combine samples, acidify (5% FA) and...
steps.instruction: Combine samples, acidify (5% FA) and speed-vac to dryness (gel like consistency).

steps.title: Resuspend samples in 5% FA, 5% ACN
steps.instruction: Resuspend samples in 5% FA, 5% ACN.

steps.title: Perform C-18 cleanup:
steps.instruction: Perform C-18 cleanup:

steps.title: h
steps.instruction: h. Elute with 2 x 500 µL 75% ACN/1 % FA.

steps.title: f
steps.instruction: f. Wash with 3 mL of 1% FA/5% ACN.

steps.title: Dry down in speedvac
steps.instruction: Dry down in speedvac.

steps.title: Resuspend samples in 5% FA, 5% ACN
steps.instruction: Resuspend samples in 5% FA, 5% ACN.

steps.title: Perform six C18-based stage-tips (one per fraction)
steps.instruction: Perform six C18-based stage-tips (one per fraction).

steps.title: a
steps.instruction: a. Wash C-18 with 50 µL 100% ACN. Centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 at Room temperature , discard flowthrough.

steps.title: h
steps.instruction: h. Elute with 1 x 50 µL 75% ACN/% FA, in mass-spec vial. Centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: f
steps.instruction: f. Wash with 50 µL of 1% FA/5% ACN. Centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 at Room temperature , discard flowthrough.

steps.title: d
steps.instruction: d. Load sample. Centrifuge at 1500 x g, °C, for 00:04:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: b
steps.instruction: b. Equilibrate with 50 µL of 1% FA. Centrifuge at 2000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 at Room temperature , discard flowthrough.

steps.title: Dry down in speedvac
steps.instruction: Dry down in speedvac.

steps.title: Resuspend in 10 µL 5% FA, 5% ACN
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 10 µL 5% FA, 5% ACN.

steps.title: Search raw data against UniProt human...
steps.instruction: Search raw data against UniProt human protein database using any proteomic analysis software with the following parameters: Up to 3 missed cleavages allowed for trypsin/LysC digestion. Carbamidomethyl (C), TMT (N-term peptide and K) set as a fixed modification. Oxidation (M) and di-glycine (K) set as variable modifications.

steps.title: Extract signal to noise intensity values...
steps.instruction: Extract signal to noise intensity values of each TMT reporter and identified proteins, and further calculate the ratio of each condition to the control sample’s intensity.

steps.title: Collect mass spectrometry data using an...
steps.instruction: Collect mass spectrometry data using an Orbitrap Fusion Lumos mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA) coupled to a Proxeon EASY-nLC1200 liquid chromatography (LC) pump (Thermo Fisher Scientific).

steps.title: For analysis, load 1/3 of each fraction onto the column
steps.instruction: For analysis, load 1/3 of each fraction onto the column. Each analysis used the Multi-Notch MS3-based TMT method (McAlister et al., 2014). The scan sequence began with an MS1 spectrum (Orbitrap analysis; resolution 120,000 at 200 Th; mass range 400-1250 m/z; automatic gain control (AGC) target 1×106; maximum injection time 100 ms).

steps.title: Select precursors for MS2 analysis using...
steps.instruction: Select precursors for MS2 analysis using a Top 4 sec method. MS2 analysis consisted of collision-induced dissociation (quadrupole Orbitrap analysis; AGC 1×105; isolation window 0.7 Th; normalized collision energy (NCE) 35; maximum injection time 300 ms resolution was 7,500 at 200 Th)

steps.title: Use monoisotopic peak assignment, and exclude...
steps.instruction: Use monoisotopic peak assignment, and exclude previously interrogated precursors using a dynamic window (120 s ± 7 ppm).

steps.title: As described previously, select only precursors...
steps.instruction: As described previously, select only precursors with a charge state between 3 and 6 for downstream analysis (Rose et al., 2016).

steps.title: Following acquisition of each MS2 spectrum,...
steps.instruction: Following acquisition of each MS2 spectrum, collect a synchronous-precursor-selection (SPS) MS3 scan on the top 10 most intense ions in the MS2 spectrum (McAlister et al., 2014).

steps.title: Fragment MS3 precursors by high energy...
steps.instruction: Fragment MS3 precursors by high energy collision-induced dissociation (HCD) and analyze using the Orbitrap (NCE 65; AGC 2×10 5 ; maximum injection time 500 ms, resolution was 50,000 at 200 Th).
